title: Multi-horizon auxiliary targets instead of single-step-only learning
summary: A recent lap-level F1 pit-stop paper uses multi-task learning; for this tabular setup, the simpler analogue is to derive auxiliary labels such as stop within 2 laps, within 3 laps, and remaining laps in stint.
rationale: A recent lap-level F1 pit-stop paper uses multi-task learning; for this tabular setup, the simpler analogue is to derive auxiliary labels such as stop within 2 laps, within 3 laps, and remaining laps in stint. Those tasks are denser and can regularize the brittle next-lap label.
implementation_hint: Create fold-safe auxiliary targets from each race sequence: `pit_in_2`, `pit_in_3`, and discrete/continuous remaining-stint labels. Train separate models or a shared stack of models on these targets, then feed their OOF predictions into the final `PitNextLap` blender. Judge lift only under forward/embargoed CV because nearby horizons are highly correlated.
expected_effect: Often stabilizes predictions near the decision boundary and can recover recall on emerging pit windows without overreacting to one noisy lap.
risk: This can collapse into target duplication if not evaluated carefully; gains may be small if the final model already infers short-horizon hazard well.
